summary: Day-2 VPC operations: self-service tagging of subnets and route tables, guardrailed DNS-hostname toggling and bounded S3/DynamoDB gateway endpoints, and engineer-only authoring or removal of network ACL rules. Structural changes — new VPCs or subnets, interface endpoints, route-table associations, full NACL creation — stay engineer-only in the separate legacy VPC/routing catalog.

operations.title: Update subnet tags
operations.summary: Add or update a tag on this subnet for tracking and cost reporting. No effect on routing or reachability.
operations.description: Add or change a tag (e.g. cost-centre, application, environment) on an existing subnet for inventory and cost-allocation accuracy. Tag-only edit with no effect on routing, NACLs, or reachability.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet
params.help: The subnet to tag

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key, max 128 characters

params.name: new_tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: New value for the tag, max 256 characters

operations.title: Update route table tags
operations.summary: Add or update a tag on this route table for tracking and cost reporting. Routes and subnet links are untouched.
operations.description: Add or change a tag on an existing route table for inventory and cost-allocation purposes. Does not touch routes or subnet associations.

params.name: route_table
params.label: Route table
params.help: The route table to tag

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key, max 128 characters

params.name: new_tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: New value for the tag, max 256 characters

operations.title: Toggle DNS hostnames for a VPC
operations.summary: Turn automatic DNS hostnames on or off for this VPC; turning it off can break hostname-based connections. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Enable or disable automatic DNS hostname assignment (enable_dns_hostnames) for a VPC. Disabling can break hostname-based resolution that application and database servers rely on for AD/SSM connectivity, so this needs a peer review before applying.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC
params.help: The VPC to change

params.name: new_enable_dns_hostnames
params.label: Enable DNS hostnames
params.help: true assigns DNS hostnames to instances launched in the VPC; false disables it

operations.title: Add an S3 or DynamoDB gateway VPC endpoint
operations.summary: Add a private connection so S3 or DynamoDB traffic skips the internet and NAT gateway. Reviewed before applying.
operations.description: Create a new gateway VPC endpoint for S3 or DynamoDB in an existing VPC and attach it to a route table, keeping that traffic off the NAT gateway/internet path. Limited to the two AWS-managed gateway services; interface endpoints and arbitrary service names remain engineer-authored elsewhere in this estate.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC
params.help: VPC the endpoint will be created in

params.name: service
params.label: Endpoint service
params.help: Only S3 and DynamoDB gateway endpoints are self-serviceable

params.name: route_table
params.label: Route table to attach
params.help: Existing route table that receives the endpoint's prefix-list route

params.name: name_tag
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Optional Name tag for the new endpoint

operations.title: Add a network ACL rule
operations.summary: Add a firewall rule to this network ACL; a mistake can open or block traffic for an entire subnet, so an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Append a new ingress or egress rule (rule number, protocol, action, CIDR, port range) to an existing network ACL. NACL rules are stateless subnet-level firewall entries and rule-number ordering matters; an incorrectly authored rule can open or block an entire subnet's traffic, so this is engineer-authored and reviewed.

params.name: nacl
params.label: Network ACL
params.help: The NACL to add a rule to

params.name: direction
params.label: Direction
params.help: Whether the rule applies to inbound or outbound traffic

params.name: rule_no
params.label: Rule number
params.help: Evaluation order; lower numbers are evaluated first and must not collide with an existing rule

params.name: protocol
params.label: Protocol
params.help: -1 means all protocols

params.name: rule_action
params.label: Action
params.help: Whether traffic matching this rule is permitted or blocked; AWS network ACL entries support exactly two actions, so the allow/deny allowlist covers the complete valid set.

params.name: cidr_block
params.label: CIDR block
params.help: Source/destination CIDR for the rule; restricted to internal ranges

params.name: from_port
params.label: From port
params.help: Start of port range (0 for all ports on ICMP/-1)

params.name: to_port
params.label: To port
params.help: End of port range

operations.title: Remove a network ACL rule
operations.summary: Remove a firewall rule from this network ACL; this can immediately open or close traffic for every subnet it protects. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Delete an existing ingress or egress rule from a network ACL by rule number. Removing a rule can immediately open or close traffic for every subnet associated with that NACL, so this is engineer-authored and reviewed.

params.name: nacl
params.label: Network ACL
params.help: The NACL to remove a rule from

params.name: direction
params.label: Direction
params.help: Whether the rule being removed sits in the NACL's inbound (ingress) or outbound (egress) list; NACLs keep the two lists separate, so the rule number alone does not identify a rule without this.

params.name: rule_no
params.label: Rule number
params.help: Rule number to remove

operations.title: Add a tag to the default network ACL
operations.summary: Add a tag to the default network ACL. Metadata only - no effect on traffic filtering.
operations.description: Add a single key/value entry to the tags map of an aws_default_network_acl. Metadata only — no effect on traffic filtering. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: network_acl
params.label: Default network ACL
params.help: The default network ACL that receives the tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_default_network_acl can be selected; tagging never changes traffic filtering.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1–128 chars: letters, digits, spaces and + - = . _ : / @

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0–256 chars: letters, digits, spaces and + - = . _ : / @

operations.title: Remove a tag from the default network ACL
operations.summary: Remove a tag from the default network ACL. Metadata only - no effect on traffic filtering.
operations.description: Remove a key from the tags map of an aws_default_network_acl. Metadata only — no effect on traffic filtering. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: network_acl
params.label: Default network ACL
params.help: The default network ACL whose tag is removed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_default_network_acl can be selected; removing a tag never changes traffic filtering.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of an existing tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Associate a subnet with the default network ACL
operations.summary: Move a subnet under the default network ACL's protection; this changes which firewall filters its traffic. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Add one subnet to the subnet_ids list of an aws_default_network_acl so that subnet is filtered by this ACL. Changes which stateless firewall applies to the subnet, so it is approval-gated. Reversible; no resource restart.

params.name: network_acl
params.label: Default network ACL
params.help: The default network ACL that will start filtering the chosen subnet's traffic, selected from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_default_network_acl can be targeted.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet to associate
params.help: Literal subnet ID like subnet-0abc12345def67890, matching how the estate authors these association lists — copy it from the ticket or the subnet's page.

operations.title: Disassociate a subnet from the default network ACL
operations.summary: Move a subnet off the default network ACL's protection; this changes which firewall filters its traffic. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Remove one subnet from the subnet_ids list of an aws_default_network_acl. Changes which stateless firewall applies to the subnet, so it is approval-gated. Reversible; no resource restart.

params.name: network_acl
params.label: Default network ACL
params.help: The default network ACL the subnet is being removed from, selected from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_default_network_acl can be targeted.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet to disassociate
params.help: Literal subnet ID like subnet-0abc12345def67890, matching how the estate authors these association lists — copy it from the ticket or the subnet's page.

operations.title: Request another change to default network ACL
operations.summary: Request any other change to the default network ACL; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to an aws_default_network_acl not offered as a bounded self-service form — e.g. editing ingress/egress rule fields, protocol/port ranges, or ForceNew attributes. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: network_acl
params.label: Default network ACL
params.help: The default network ACL to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Add a tag to an Elastic IP
operations.summary: Add a tag to this Elastic IP. Metadata only - the address and its association are unaffected.
operations.description: Append one key/value entry to the aws_eip tags map. Cosmetic metadata only; fully reversible, no downtime, no replacement.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: Elastic IP that receives the new tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_eip can be modified; tags are metadata and never change the address or its association.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key: up to 128 chars from letters, numbers, spaces and _.:/=+-@

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: AWS tag value: up to 256 chars from letters, numbers, spaces and _.:/=+-@

operations.title: Remove a tag from an Elastic IP
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this Elastic IP. Metadata only - the address and its association are unaffected.
operations.description: Remove one key from the aws_eip tags map by key. Cosmetic metadata only; fully reversible, no downtime, no replacement.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: Elastic IP whose tag is removed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_eip can be modified; removing a tag never changes the address or its association.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of the existing tag entry to drop.

operations.title: Associate Elastic IP with an EC2 instance
operations.summary: Point this Elastic IP at a server, giving it a public address; reviewed first since it changes internet exposure.
operations.description: Point an allocated Elastic IP at an EC2 instance by setting `instance`. Provider re-associates in place (no replacement). Reversible; assigns a public IP so it changes internet exposure — guardrail/approval required.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: The already-allocated Elastic IP to attach, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_eip — never an arbitrary public address — can be pointed at an instance.

params.name: instance
params.label: Target EC2 instance
params.help: Instance to receive the Elastic IP. Cannot be set together with network_interface.

operations.title: Associate Elastic IP with a network interface
operations.summary: Point this Elastic IP at a network interface, giving it a public address; reviewed first since it changes internet exposure.
operations.description: Point an allocated Elastic IP at an elastic network interface by setting `network_interface`. Provider re-associates in place (no replacement). Reversible; assigns a public IP so it changes internet exposure — guardrail/approval required.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: The already-allocated Elastic IP to attach, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_eip — never an arbitrary public address — can be pointed at a network interface.

params.name: network_interface
params.label: Target network interface
params.help: ENI to receive the Elastic IP. Cannot be set together with instance.

operations.title: Disassociate an Elastic IP
operations.summary: Detach this Elastic IP from whatever it's attached to; anything relying on that public address briefly loses reachability. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Detach the Elastic IP from whatever it points at by clearing the chosen association attribute. Updates in place (no replacement) and is reversible, but severs public reachability so anything depending on the public IP sees a brief interruption — guardrail/approval required.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: Elastic IP whose association is being cleared, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_eip can be detached; the address itself stays allocated to the account and can be re-associated later.

params.name: detach_target
params.label: Association to clear
params.help: Which association attribute to null out on this Elastic IP.

operations.title: Request another change to Elastic IP
operations.summary: Request any other change to this Elastic IP; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Catch-all for any Elastic IP change not offered as a bounded form — for example allocation-time attributes that force replacement (address, domain, public_ipv4_pool, customer_owned_ipv4_pool, network_border_group, ipam_pool_id) or associate_with_private_ip. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: eip
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: The Elastic IP to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Provision a new VPC
operations.summary: Request a brand-new isolated network; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new VPC as a complete specification: its primary IPv4 CIDR block and DNS settings. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Subnets, gateways and route tables are separate follow-up steps once the VPC exists.

params.name: vpc_name
params.label: VPC name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the VPC in the generated draft — a VPC has no name field of its own.

params.name: cidr_block
params.label: Primary IPv4 CIDR block

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only — RFC1918, no public range, no 0.0.0.0/0

params.help: e.g. 10.3.0.0/16 — internal ranges only; a public block is never self-service.

params.name: enable_dns_support
params.label: DNS resolution
params.help: Always on for a new VPC — required for private DNS and VPC endpoints, written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: enable_dns_hostnames
params.label: DNS hostnames
params.help: Assigns DNS hostnames to instances launched in the VPC. Can be changed later with the existing toggle action.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: VPC name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this VPC is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this VPC.

decisions: Instance tenancy defaults to shared; dedicated tenancy is an engineer decision
IPv6 CIDR association, IPAM pool sourcing, and network-address-usage metrics are engineer decisions
Subnets, an internet gateway, NAT gateways and route tables are created as separate follow-up steps — an empty VPC has no reachability of its own either way

operations.title: Provision a new subnet
operations.summary: Request a brand-new subnet inside an existing VPC; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new subnet as a complete specification: its VPC, CIDR block, Availability Zone, and whether it auto-assigns public IPs. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: subnet_name
params.label: Subnet name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the subnet in the generated draft — a subnet has no name field of its own.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC
params.help: The VPC this subnet is carved out of.

params.name: cidr_block
params.label: IPv4 CIDR block

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only, must sit inside the chosen VPC's CIDR

params.help: Must be a range inside the VPC's own CIDR block and not overlap an existing subnet.

params.name: availability_zone
params.label: Availability Zone
params.help: e.g. ap-southeast-5a — the zone this subnet lives in.

params.name: map_public_ip_on_launch
params.label: Auto-assign public IPv4
params.help: Off by default (private subnet). On means every instance launched here without an explicit override receives a public IP.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Subnet name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this subnet is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this subnet.

decisions: Auto-assign public IPv4 defaults to off; turning it on means this subnet is meant to be internet-facing — confirm it will have a route to an internet gateway before flipping it, and that this is the intended reachability change
Route table association is a separate follow-up step (the routing service's associate-a-subnet action) — an unassociated subnet uses the VPC's main route table
IPv6 CIDR association, customer-owned IP pools, and outpost placement are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new internet gateway
operations.summary: Request a brand-new internet gateway attached to a VPC; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new internet gateway and attach it to an existing VPC. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Attaching it is what makes a public route (0.0.0.0/0 via this gateway) possible; it is not reachable until a route table actually points at it.

params.name: igw_name
params.label: Internet gateway name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the gateway in the generated draft — an internet gateway has no name field of its own.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC to attach
params.help: The VPC this gateway attaches to.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Internet gateway name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this gateway is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this gateway.

decisions: Attaching an internet gateway to a VPC is what makes a public route to 0.0.0.0/0 possible for any subnet whose route table points at it — confirm that reachability change is intended before it is wired into a route table
Pointing a route table at this gateway is a separate follow-up step (the routing service's add-a-route action) — attaching alone does not make any subnet reachable

operations.title: Provision a new NAT gateway
operations.summary: Request a brand-new NAT gateway in a subnet; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new NAT gateway as a complete specification: its subnet, connectivity type, and (for a public gateway) the Elastic IP it uses. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: nat_name
params.label: NAT gateway name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the gateway in the generated draft — a NAT gateway has no name field of its own.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet
params.help: The subnet this NAT gateway lives in — public for a public gateway, private for a private one.

params.name: connectivity_type
params.label: Connectivity type
params.help: public routes outbound traffic to the internet via an Elastic IP; private only routes between VPCs/on-prem.

params.name: allocation
params.label: Elastic IP
params.help: Required for a public NAT gateway — the already-allocated address it advertises. Not used for a private gateway.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: NAT gateway name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this gateway is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this gateway.

decisions: Secondary allocation IDs and secondary private IP address ranges are engineer decisions
Pointing a private subnet's route table at this gateway is a separate follow-up step (the routing service's add-a-route action)

operations.title: Provision a new Elastic IP
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Elastic IP address; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Allocate a new Elastic IP address, unassociated. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Associating it with an instance, network interface, or NAT gateway is a separate follow-up step.

params.name: eip_name
params.label: Elastic IP name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the address in the generated draft — an Elastic IP has no name field of its own.

params.name: domain
params.label: Domain
params.help: Always vpc — EC2-Classic is retired, written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Elastic IP name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this address is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this address.

decisions: Associating this address with an instance, network interface, or NAT gateway is a separate follow-up step (the existing associate actions)
A specific public IPv4 pool, customer-owned IP pool, or fixed network border group are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new VPC endpoint
operations.summary: Request a brand-new VPC endpoint (Gateway or Interface); an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new VPC endpoint as a complete specification: its type, target service, and network placement. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Broader than the existing self-service S3/DynamoDB gateway-endpoint action: this form also covers Interface endpoints and other AWS-managed gateway services.

params.name: endpoint_name
params.label: Endpoint name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the endpoint in the generated draft — a VPC endpoint has no name field of its own.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC
params.help: The VPC this endpoint serves.

params.name: vpc_endpoint_type
params.label: Endpoint type
params.help: Gateway endpoints (S3, DynamoDB) attach to route tables; Interface endpoints place an ENI in chosen subnets.

params.name: service_name
params.label: Service name
params.help: The full AWS PrivateLink service name, e.g. com.amazonaws.ap-southeast-5.ssm.

params.name: route_tables
params.label: Route tables to attach
params.help: Required for a Gateway endpoint — every route table that should receive its prefix-list route (typically every private route table that needs this path).

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Required for an Interface endpoint — one subnet per Availability Zone to place its network interfaces in.

params.name: private_dns_enabled
params.label: Private DNS
params.help: On resolves the service's public DNS name to the endpoint's private IP inside this VPC — the common case for Interface endpoints.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Required for an Interface endpoint — who can reach its network interface. Pick one to five groups.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: VPC endpoint name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this endpoint is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this endpoint.

decisions: An Interface endpoint's security groups gate who can reach it; confirm the chosen groups match the intended reachability before this endpoint goes live — a permissive choice quietly opens a private-network shortcut to the service
For the S3/DynamoDB-only self-service path, the existing add-a-gateway-endpoint action stays available; use this form for Interface endpoints or other gateway services
A resource policy restricting who may use the endpoint, and IP address type, are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new VPC peering connection
operations.summary: Request a brand-new VPC peering connection; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new VPC peering connection between this account's VPC and a peer VPC. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. A peering connection alone routes nothing; the route table entries on both sides are a required follow-up.

params.name: peering_name
params.label: Peering connection name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the connection in the generated draft — a peering connection has no name field of its own.

params.name: vpc
params.label: Requester VPC
params.help: This account's VPC that initiates the peering request.

params.name: peer_vpc_id
params.label: Peer VPC ID
params.help: Literal VPC ID of the peer, e.g. vpc-0abc12345def67890 — copy it from the ticket or the peer account's console; a same-account peer would instead be picked from inventory, so this stays a literal ID for the common cross-account/cross-region case.

params.name: peer_region
params.label: Peer region
params.help: Only needed for a cross-region peering; leave blank for a peer VPC in this same region.

params.name: auto_accept
params.label: Auto-accept
params.help: Never auto-accepted — a cross-account or cross-region peer must accept explicitly, written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Peering connection name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this connection is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this connection.

decisions: Peering two VPCs whose CIDR blocks are not disjoint fails at apply — confirm the ranges do not overlap before submitting
Route table entries on BOTH sides pointing at the peering connection are a required follow-up (the routing service's add-a-route action) — an unrouted peering connection reaches nothing
DNS resolution across the peering (allow_remote_vpc_dns_resolution on the accepter/requester blocks) is an engineer decision
